description_long: This guard stone is a vertically elongated, sculpted stone slab featuring a relief of a robed human figure holding a shield. The figure is carved in high relief, with detailed drapery folds and a rectangular background panel framing the upper body. The edges of the stone are rough and irregular, suggesting it was once part of a larger architectural element or has suffered breakage. The back of the stone is mostly flat, with the relief only present on the front face. The surface texture appears weathered, with some chipping and erosion along the sides, likely from prolonged outdoor exposure. The overall appearance suggests historical or decorative significance, possibly serving as both a protective barrier and a commemorative marker.
description: A tall, weathered stone slab with a high-relief carving of a robed figure holding a shield, featuring rough edges and a flat back, likely used as a historical guard stone.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full relief of the robed figure with shield, detailed drapery, and rectangular background panel.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thinness of the slab and the high relief of the figure projecting from the flat back.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the mostly flat back surface and the reverse side of the relief, with minimal detail.
description_view_3: Opposite side view highlighting the depth of the relief and the irregular, chipped edge of the stone.
attribute_location: Typically found at the corners of historical buildings, entrances to courtyards, or along the edges of old urban streets, often marking boundaries or protecting structures from vehicle impact.
attribute_installation_orientation: Meant to be installed upright, partially embedded in the ground for stability, with the relief facing outward toward the street or pathway.
attribute_wear_patterns: Visible chipping and erosion along the edges, especially on the sides exposed to traffic or weather. The relief details are smoothed in some areas, likely from prolonged exposure to rain, wind, and occasional contact with vehicles or pedestrians.
